title: Ensure that the Container Network Interface file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the Container Network Interface file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the Container Network Interface files have ownership set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: Container Network Interface provides various networking options for overlay networking. You should consult their documentation and restrict their respective file permissions to maintain the integrity of those files. Those files should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the master node. For example, stat -c %U:%G <path/to/cni/files> Verify that the ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the master node. For example, chown root:root <path/to/cni/files>

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the master node where the Container Network Interface (CNI) files are located.
2. Navigate to the directory where the CNI files are stored. This is typically found in `/etc/cni/net.d/` or a similar path depending on your Kubernetes setup.
3. Run the command: `stat -c %U:%G <path/to/cni/files>` for each CNI file to check the ownership.
4. Verify that the ownership is set to `root:root`.
5. If any file is not owned by `root:root`, run the command: `chown root:root <path/to/cni/files>` to change the ownership.

**Expected secure configuration:** All CNI files should have ownership set to `root:root`.

**How to confirm compliance:** If all CNI files are owned by `root:root`, the control is compliant. If any files are not owned by `root:root`, the control is non-compliant.
